outages.headline: Russia’s fuel market shows partial recovery, deputy PM says
outages.summary: Russia’s domestic fuel market has shown signs of partial stabilization, Deputy Prime Minister Alexander Novak said Tuesday. He noted that conditions remain challenging in certain regions. Ukrainian drone strikes on Russian refineries in recent months disrupted fuel supplies across the country. The attacks led to long lines at gas stations, elevated gasoline prices, and fuel ...

outages.headline: Houthi Threats Ignite New Oil Price Surge
outages.summary: Houthi attacks are expanding the Middle East conflict into the Red Sea, forcing tankers to reroute and pushing Brent above $91 amid growing fears of prolonged supply disruptions. Aramco Floods the Red Sea With Crude Ahead of Houthi Escalation - Perhaps foreshadowing an escalation in the Persian Gulf, Saudi Arabia’s national oil company Saudi Aramco shipped record volumes of crude from its Red Sea port of Yanbu over the past four weeks. - Yemen’s Houthi rebels have sent an email to most global shipping companies, warning against loading…

outages.headline: CPC suspends new Kaohsiung LSMGO nominations amid tight barge schedule
outages.summary: State-owned bunker supplier CPC Corporation (CPC) has temporarily stopped accepting new LSMGO nominations for deliveries at Taiwan’s Kaohsiung port due to a tight barge schedule. LSMGO deliveries have been suspended since last Friday and will remain on hold until CPC clears its backlog of pending orders, according to a source. The disruption has also affected ...

outages.headline: Geopolitical Disruption Reduces Cargo Volume at Port of Antwerp
